title: How necessary is the brain CNS for an EVA to function
display_title: How necessary is the brain CNS for an EVA to function
wikitext:
[]: How necessary is the brain/CNS for an EVA to function             Quote Post by hwdge » Thu Dec 13, 2012 1:45 am In the 18th episode, infected Eva 03 just went limp when Eva 01 snapped its neck, but in the end of evangelion, the MP Evas function fine with their brains however messed up it may be, but in the first episode Eva 01 blanks out when it gets a spike in it's brain (or was that Shinji's sympathetic pain thing?)

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Thu Dec 13, 2012 8:46 am The key distinction here is that the MP Evas have S2 engines (Fruit of Life), which essentially grants them the full powers of the angels. None of Nerv's Evas have S2s throughout most of the series (up until Unit 01 acquires one). Also, to your point, this may have something to do with the fact that the MPEs aren't piloted by humans. There's no human child in the cockpit who will die if the Eva's neck is snapped, or if its head is lopped off.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 13, 2012 12:36 pm user-02: The S2 Engine doesn't seem to have anything to do with it. As the OP notes, Eva-03 goes limp when her neck is snapped, and she has an S2 Engine (courtesy of Bardiel). Conversely, Eva-01 is able to go berserk against Sachiel even after her braincase is pierced. I do believe that the "human pilot involvement" is the key here. (Although capable of being subverted if the relationship between pilot and Eva is right: see Asuka and Kyoko in EoE.) Evas have fully biological bodies, but their functioning is not as explicitly "bound" to their bodies' intactness as it is in mortals. As long as the core is intact, they still have a "life force", and even an extremely damaged body can continue to be animated.

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Fri Dec 14, 2012 1:20 pm I was under the impression that the core functioned as more of a brain than the actual brain, hence why it is such a target in defeating the angels (BOOM! Headshot! of sorts) and why when it was exposed, Zeruel began attacking Unit-01's core specifically. It serves as an insta-kill soft spot due to the fact that it holds whatever consciousness whatever being has. Further evidence of this is the fact that the entry plug has to somehow link with the core on some level, not the brain.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 14, 2012 5:22 pm Tribblepoo wrote: Further evidence of this is the fact that the entry plug has to somehow link with the core on some level, not the brain.

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Fri Dec 14, 2012 5:46 pm Reichu wrote: It links to both the core and the CNS. Synchronization has a lot of nerve-related blabber. The Evas are basically "human gods", and almost certainly created in the "image" of the First Ancestral Race. (FAR create Seeds in their image; Lilin clone the Seeds to make Evas.) As such, their physical template is that of a mortal biological creature, but the power-up to "god" renders most of these structures vestigial. The actual Eva does not need crucial things like the CNS to be intact in order to function; the core is all that matters, in the end. However, because the Eva's structure so strongly mimics that of a lowly mortal, synchronization is possible. And because the Eva pilots DO rely upon an intact body much more than an Eva does, the pilot can be easily incapacitated by vicarious injuries that the Eva herself would simply shake off.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 14, 2012 5:54 pm Tribblepoo wrote: none of the incidents really come into context until Unit-01 plays the Omnomnom Song for Zeruel.

[]:             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Mon Dec 17, 2012 1:09 am I guess they both control the Eva. When the CNS is destroyed, the Eva either shuts down or goes into berserk mode. Maybe one can be dominant over the other. I don't fully remember, but doesn't Shinji defeat unit 2 in ep 24 by prog-knifing the spinal column?
